mission.title: fluent-bit: `aws` plugin fails to retrieve IMDSv2 token to get metadata in >= 2.1.0
mission.description: `aws` plugin fails to retrieve IMDSv2 token to get metadata in >= 2.1.0. This issue affects 14+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluent-bit troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
The `aws` plugin fails to get IMDSv2 token.

**To Reproduce**
Enable the `aws` plugin and force the use of IMDSv2 on the EC2 instance.

The `aws` plugin should be able to obtain IMDS metadata without error.

steps.title: Apply the fix for `aws` plugin fails to retrieve IMDSv2 token to get metadata…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [debug] [imds] using IMDSv2
[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [trace] [upstream] get new connection for 169.254.169.254:80, net setup:
net.connect_timeout        = 1 seconds
net.source_address         = any
net.keepalive              = disabled
net.keepalive_idle_timeout = 30 seconds
[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [trace] [net] connection #46 in process to 169.254.169.254:80
[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [trace] [io] connection OK
[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [debug] [http_client] not using http_proxy for header
[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [error] [aws_client] failed to add header to request
[2023/04/27 16:07:03]
```

steps.title: Confirm `aws` plugin fails to retrieve IMDSv2 token to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/fluent/fluent-bit/issues/7282

[2023/04/27 16:07:03] [error] [filter:aws:aws.1] Failed to get instanc

[2023/04/27 18:33:27] [error] [filter:aws:aws.1] Failed to get instance ID

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
